title: High Performance Rate Limiting at Databricks
summary: In this article, we look at how Databricks implemented rate limiting at scale, how they shrank the critical path, and the accuracy tradeoff that shrinking usually requires.

title: How Figma Upgraded Data Pipeline from Multi-Day Latency to Real-Time
summary: In this article, we will learn what happened as Figma grew and how its engineering team handled the growth in terms of the data pipeline issues.

title: How Pinterest Built a Production MCP Ecosystem
summary: In this article, we look at how Pinterest designed that ecosystem and what they had to get right beyond the protocol itself.

title: EP214: Claude Code vs. OpenClaw: 5 Design Dimensions
summary: Both are highly capable, but they have key architectural differences.

title: Become an AI Engineer | Enrollment Ends Soon
summary: Our 6th cohort of Becoming an AI Engineer starts in about a week.

title: Container Design Patterns for Distributed Systems
summary: In this article, we’ll walk through the patterns that have crystallized over the past decade, organized by the scope of their coordination.

title: How Instacart Built a Search for Billions of Products
summary: In this article, we will learn how Instacart’s search infrastructure evolved over the years and the challenges its engineering team faced.

title: Connecting LLMs to the Real World: Tool Use, Function Calling, and MCP
summary: In this article, we will look at this progression that has happened from basic tool use to function calling to the Model Context Protocol, allowing the LLMs to go from isolated text generation tools to assistants that can do interesting stuff for the end users.

title: EP213: MCP vs Skills, Clearly Explained
summary: Both MCP and Skills extend what an agent can do. But they solve different problems, and picking the wrong one adds cost or complexity you don't need.

title: A Beginner’s Guide to Kubernetes
summary: In this article, we will learn how Kubernetes is a system of promises, and that every piece of it is a small program keeping one of those promises.
